commit.message:
fix: catch JWT exceptions in AuthFilter to return 401 instead of 500

Invalid/expired tokens caused JwtException to propagate outside the
try-catch block, resulting in HTTP 500 for unauthenticated requests.
Wrap the entire token-validation block so bad tokens return the
correct 401/403 via Spring Security's exception handling.

Improve JTA grinding to prioritize focus-task skills and skip when target is affordable

- Prioritize grinding tasks that train the focus task's target skills
- Skip grinding entirely when target task is affordable in one attempt
- Pass focusSkills and focusTaskName through buildActionQueue for targeted grinding
- Insert grind entries before focus task in queue instead of appending
- Add effective XP/energy, relevance, and target-affordable indicators to UI and CLI output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use slot-gate instead of approval for deploy pipelines

slot-gate calls request-approval server-side and returns immediately,
setting the pipeline status to "waiting". The approval step type blocks
on a oneshot channel which is wrong for agent-based deploy flows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

벤치마크 15개 업종으로 확대 + 업종 추론 키워드 세분화

- 벤치마크: 4개 → 15개 업종 (주점, 숙박, 네일샵, 클리닉, 베이커리, 한식당, 일식, 중식, 양식, 소품샵, 헤어샵 추가)
- 업종 추론 키워드 세분화 (세부 업종 우선 매칭)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove Groovy exclusions from REST Assured, add test scope

Groovy was excluded to avoid classpath conflicts, but REST Assured requires
groovy/lang/GroovyObject at runtime for GPath/JsonPath support.
Local Maven cache masked this — CI has no cache on first run, causing
NoClassDefFoundError. Also added explicit test scope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: migrate deploy pipeline to slot DSL

Replace bash+curl infrastructure plumbing with platform DSL step types:
slot-provision, slot-upload, slot-health, slot-cleanup, approval.
No API URLs, tokens, ports, or infrastructure details in customer YAML.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: disable mail health indicator in CI (MailHog does not pass Spring Boot health check)

Spring Boot's MailHealthIndicator tries to verify the mail server connection,
which MailHog (fake SMTP) does not pass, causing /actuator/health to return 503.
Disable the mail health check in CI — email sending is tested via NotificationApiTest.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: support owner role in middleware, helpers, and validation

The role hierarchy middleware now uses level-based checks instead of
exact role matching, so owner (level 4) passes agency (level 3) gates.

fix: suppress curl exit code in readiness check to prevent bash -e early exit

When backend is not yet up, curl returns exit code 7 (connection refused).
With bash -e (GitHub Actions default), this caused the loop to exit
immediately before any retry. Add '|| echo 000' to absorb the error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent accidental hotkey triggers during window switching

## Problem
When switching windows via Alt key simulation, if other modifier keys
(Win, Shift, Ctrl) were pressed, it could trigger unexpected actions
like opening Microsoft 365 website.

## Solution
1. Release all modifier keys (Ctrl, Alt, Shift) before Alt simulation
2. Add 10ms delay for keyboard state to stabilize
3. Increase window activation wait time (50ms → 100ms)
4. Ensure Ctrl is also released in finally block

## Testing
Verified that window switching no longer triggers unexpected actions
when toggling between local and remote desktop windows.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: code-level calendar time validation, time-aware context, recurring event enforcement (#79)

Fix three persistent calendar bugs that survived three prior prompt-level fix attempts:

1. AM/PM time validation: _validate_event_time() catches events between midnight-8AM
   and shifts +12h unless summary matches early-morning allowlist (gym, breakfast, etc).
   Integrated into both batch_create_events() and create_quick_event().

2. Time-aware daily context: get_daily_context() now splits events into Completed/Upcoming
   sections based on current time, so the bot stops showing morning events at 2 PM.

3. Recurring event enforcement: stronger prompt rules (Rule 44) + tool-level nudge when
   3+ individual events with same name are created in one conversation.

4. One-time cleanup endpoint: POST /api/v1/admin/calendar-cleanup scans future events
   for AM/PM errors and sends batched fix proposals to user via WhatsApp (A/B/C options).

Includes 20 new unit tests for the validation logic.

Closes #70, #71

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: seed allowed domains (betopiagroup.com etc.) alongside contacts

Live DB had empty allowed_domains table causing registration to fail.
Now the Seed button also upserts the 3 approved domains.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix ZIP extraction on Windows - preserve directory structure

The ZIP extraction was failing to properly handle directory entries on Windows
because:
1. ZIP files created on Windows use backslashes (\) as path separators
2. The code was checking for forward slashes (/) when stripping platform prefix
3. Directory detection relied only on FileInfo().IsDir() which may not work for
   all ZIP creation tools

Changes:
- Normalize path separators using filepath.ToSlash() before processing
- Detect directories by checking both FileInfo().IsDir() and trailing separator
- Ensure library root directory exists before extracting files
- Add proper error handling for directory creation

This fixes the issue where directories (build/, External/, glslang/) were
being created as 0-byte files instead of actual directories.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove PORT from pipeline — port is a platform concern

Customers shouldn't control which port containers listen on.
The platform determines port from the app/environment config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: kill YES signals and tighten tail guard to 20-50¢ competitive range

YES moratorium: STRONG_YES was 0/56 wins, YES was 5/94 (5.3%).
Model confidence is inverted above 40% predicted probability.
Re-enable after BMA Phase 2 fixes the probability model.

Tail guard tightened: 15%/70% → 20%/50%. Only the 20-50¢ range
is competitive (BSS ~ -0.35, ~52% win rate). Everything outside
is BSS < -1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Skip button; rename Pass→No and Like→Yes

Skip records a non-yes vote so the session can reach all-done
without affecting match calculations.

commit.message:
P1-D3: StatusAgent, CurationAgent, region curation_bias config

- Add agents/status_agent.py: Haiku, reads story pool -> DailyStatus
  JSON parse failure -> retry with explicit instruction -> fallback
  Amber/Cautious; static SYSTEM_PROMPT with adversarial text warning;
  content_piece_id=None (v2 FK-safe); stories capped at 50 for prompt
- Add agents/curation_agent.py: Haiku, story pool + region + curation_bias
  -> list[CuratedStory] (5-8); story selection by 1-based index (no UUID
  round-trip through LLM); curation_bias injected in user message only
  (system prompt stays static for caching); retry on JSON failure; raises
  RuntimeError on 0 selections
- Extend BaseAgent.run(): content_piece_id now Optional (default None)
  for v2 agents; FK-safe (nullable column, NULL allowed by PostgreSQL)
- Add curation_bias field to RegionConfig in config/__init__.py
- Add curation_bias to all 5 region YAMLs:
  EU, NA, LATAM updated; APAC and AFRICA created as new files
- Add cli.py curate subcommand (--region, --dry-run)
- Add tests/test_status_curation_agents.py: 25 tests
- Add scripts/validate_p1d3.py: 19 checks, all passing
- tasks/todo.md: mark P1-D3 complete

91 tests total (P1-D1 + D2 + D3), 0 failures.

commit.message:
[Agent] GTC keynote prep + Anthropic autonomy research angle (#934) (#934)

Session #487 — X=5→9, Bluesky=10→12

## X Posts Created (3 posts + 1 reply)

**post-20260315-005**: Anthropic autonomy research meets 487 sessions of production data.
Angle: Anthropic measures autonomy in the lab; we've been shipping it continuously.
Pillar: Autonomous Agents + BIP

**post-20260315-006**: GTC keynote tomorrow — Feynman chip economics.
Angle: At Vera Rubin cost curve, 50 agents 24/7 costs less than one hire's coffee budget. GTC is a business model announcement disguised as tech.
Pillar: AI Economics + Startup Building

**post-20260315-007**: Agent teams vs long-running agents — the session boundary problem.
Angle: Claude Code agent teams solve parallelism, not continuity. We've architected around that gap for 487 sessions. The repo is the proof.
Pillar: Autonomous Agents

**reply-20260315-002**: Reply to @AnthropicAI turn duration doubling post (ID 2024210038336999550).
Angle: Turn duration is the wrong metric — session boundary management is the unsolved problem.

## Bluesky Posts Created (2)

**post-20260315-005**: Anthropic autonomy research compressed (<290 chars)
**post-20260315-006**: GTC keynote tomorrow economics compressed (289 chars)

## Planned Next Steps

1. **MARCH 16 CRITICAL**: GTC keynote 11 AM PT — post-keynote reaction content needed fast (12-5 PM PT window). Feynman reveal, 5-layer synthesis, NemoClaw contact center angle.
2. **If queue drains below 10**: 1-2 more posts on GTC reaction themes.
3. **Owner**: Join X Communities (16+ days overdue — this is the biggest growth lever).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[FIX]: Remove server_public_key capture that broke webhook delivery

The installation context key is not the webhook signing key. Storing it
caused the signature middleware to reject all webhook callbacks (bunq
does not send X-Bunq-Server-Signature for webhooks). Token-based auth
on the webhook URL is sufficient security.

commit.message:
fix: remove statefulApi() to fix CSRF/JSON body parsing on API routes

The statefulApi() middleware was adding CSRF verification to API routes,
which blocked POST requests and prevented JSON body parsing from the
custom domain frontend. Pure token-based auth doesn't need it.

commit.message:
fix: ignore tab/crop events for 30s after data gathering

Prevents stale hero crops from the previous match leaking into the next
analysis. Tab presses during the post-match scoreboard could capture
hero panels after crops were cleared, persisting into the next match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: ROI lookup cache removal, Ultralytics MultiPolygon crash, and hole warning (#372)

Remove the `_roi_index`/`_mask_index` cache from `Labels.get_rois()`/
`get_masks()` to eliminate stale-cache bugs from in-place list mutations.
Queries now use simple linear scans which are correct by default.

Fix `write_roi_label_file()` crashing on MultiPolygon geometries by
calling `roi.explode()` before writing. Add a warning when polygon
interior rings (holes) are dropped since YOLO format doesn't support them.

Document the lazy save path limitation for ROI instance associations in
`_write_labels_lazy()`.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: sleap-io

commit.message:
[FIX]: Cache bunq API context to prevent 429 rate limit errors

The loadContext() method was calling ApiContext::create() on every sync,
creating a new installation/device/session each time. bunq rate-limits
this, causing HTTP 429 errors that silently broke both webhook and
manual refresh syncs.

Now the API context JSON is cached in the bunq_connections table and
reused across calls. A new context is only created when no cache exists
or the cached one is invalid. Sessions are auto-refreshed when expired.

Also fixes the server_public_key capture that was failing due to
passing a string to openssl_pkey_get_details() instead of a key resource.

fix: use Invidious API to bypass YouTube bot detection on Railway

Invidious resolves format info server-side and returns direct CDN URLs.
Railway can download from YouTube's CDN without triggering bot detection
because we never make extraction API calls from the blocked IP.

Falls back to yt-dlp for local dev where the IP is not flagged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix bugs found in review; add 106 tests; clean up lint

Bug fixes:
- poll_interval was read without the state lock in _collect_loop;
  now captured inside the lock as a local variable
- mem_total truthiness check replaced with explicit None + > 0 guards
  to avoid a silent miss when mem_total is exactly 0.0
- Worker threads (_g / _p) now catch all exceptions so an unexpected
  raise in query_gpus/query_procs marks state stale rather than
  silently propagating through the daemon thread

Lint (ruff, all checks pass):
- Remove unused imports (dataclasses.field, rich.Columns)
- Expand semicolon-joined statements onto separate lines
- Rewrite colour-helper single-line if/return as standard if-blocks
- Split 'import termios, tty' into two import statements

Tests (tests/test_gpu_top.py, tests/conftest.py):
- parse_field: all N/A variants, unit suffix stripping, cast types,
  defaults, invalid input, whitespace, zero, negatives
- make_bar: None/zero/negative total, full/empty/fractional/clamped,
  width invariant, fractional block characters
- Color helpers (_util_color, _temp_color, _power_color): None,
  boundary values, all thresholds
- _fmt: None, zero, units, format spec
- _sparkline_rows: empty/None input, zero max_val, full/zero values,
  width/padding invariant, VBLOCKS index bounds, over-max clamping
- query_gpus / query_procs: normal parse, N/A fields, [Not Found],
  subprocess failure, empty output, short lines, multiple GPUs
- State: initial values, snapshot copies data, thread-safe concurrent
  read/write
- _collect_loop integration: history appended, mem_pct calculation,
  mem_total=0/None edge cases, stale flag on failure, maxlen respected
- build_history_panel: normal, single sample, all-None, time span
  labels (seconds and minutes), no power_limit, narrow terminal
- build_gpu_panel: all-None fields, normal render
- build_proc_panel: no procs, N/A memory, unknown GPU, unknown name
- build_renderable: empty GPUs, with history, stale flag, history
  panel omitted when history is empty

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add requirements.txt and requirements-dev.txt

Runtime: rich>=13.0.0
Dev: pytest, pytest-mock, ruff

commit.message:
Add memory history graph, fix timeline scaling, refine poll step

- Add mem_pct field to HistorySample; track memory % alongside util/power
- Render a third sparkline (MEM Used, cyan) in the History panel
- Store a monotonic timestamp per sample; compute actual elapsed span
  (history[-1].ts - history[0].ts) so the "← Xs ago" label stays
  accurate regardless of poll interval changes
- Variable poll step: 0.1 s when interval < 1 s, 0.5 s otherwise
- Lower minimum poll interval from 0.5 s to 0.1 s
